import health standards is a big part in that,
but they feed into the quarantine requirements
and treatments and so on.
So yes, that is, that is the work
and the program that we have underway, uh, across that end
to end, we are the efficiencies, uh,
that we can make some fundamental performance.
Mm-hmm. Yeah. Alright. We've got time for
One last question from Mark Cameron.
Make a quick mark. We're running outta
Time. Thanks. Very quickly,
Stuart,
how do you square away the criticism from the industry
or someone in the industry that has said that it's an
exorbitant cost and it's gonna add significant levies
that will only stifle innovation
there in, how do you square that?
Yeah, look, uh, no thanks, mark.
Um, and, and look, as I said, you know, we are sympathetic
to the costs on users of this facility, uh, for quarantine.
Um, uh, you know, the, the, the, it's, it's, it's tricky.
You know, there, there's the balance of obviously
what we're trying to protect the cost
of these facilities to actually have them.
Hence there aren't private sector operators of them.
Uh, so what, you know, what, what we can do best there,
mark, is the things we've, we've talked about, um, to start
with currently as things stand without changing anything,
you know, we are subsidizing 50% of the cost to the users.
Mm-hmm. Um, then the things like bringing the time down, um,
if we can, if, if we are able to, to verify
and certify more offshore facilities.
Absolutely. And then these changes to the system whereby,
um, uh, you know, some of the requirements, uh,
could be amended that either again, make it quicker,
shorter, easier to get through, um,
or with new, new, new diagnostics and testing.
So there's a suite of things in there.
There isn't a silver bullet.
Um, but we are committed to working through, uh,
and with industry
and with all the stakeholders
to actually just get this working the most efficient way it
can, balancing why we need it
and what we're trying to protect as well.
Thanks, Stuart. Very grateful. Thanks very much.
Um, we've come to time, so I appreciate, um,
you taking the time to come and see us.
We may have a couple of written questions perhaps. Sure,
Absolutely. Yeah.
Yeah. But thank you very much. Okay.
